title: $keytype
text: $keytype Return the type of the specified key. $keytype ( Entity , KeyNumber ) Example: if ($keytype("CUSTOMER", 2) != "I") ... Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Entity String Entity name. KeyNumber Numeric Key identifier; the value will be truncated to form an integer. 1, the primary key. 2, 3, 4, and so on, the number that identifies a candidate key that has been defined for Entity . Return Values Table 2. Values returned in $keytype Value Meaning "" An error occurred. $procerror contains the exact error. P KeyNumber is a primary key (that is, KeyNumber is 1). C KeyNumber is a candidate key. I KeyNumber is an index. Table 3. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following this Function Value Error constant Meaning -1102 <UPROCERR_ENTITY> The entity name provided is not a valid name or the entity is not present in the component structure. -1104 <UPROCERR_KEY> The key number provided is not valid; for example, the key number was out of range. Use Allowed in all component types. Example: Defining the Validation for Specific Keys The following example uses the $keytype function to define the validation for specific keys in the validateKey trigger. (The validateKey trigger is activated only for primary and candidate keys.) trigger validateKey selectcase $keytype("MYENTITY", $curkey) case "P" ; perform validation for the primary key ... case "C" ; perform validation for all candidate keys ... elsecase ; oops message "Cannot validate this key" message "Context: %%$dataerrorcontext" endselectcase end; validateKey Copy Related Links Validate Determines whether the valerr data processing attribute is added for the selected entity node in the DTD tree. This attribute holds the occurrence or field validation error (depending on whether the selected item is an entity or field). Valid values are True or False (default). validatefield Validate a field of the current occurrence. validatekey Validate a key of the current occurrence. validateocc Validate an occurrence. $dataerrorcontext Return the context of the last validation error. $keymod Return a value indicating whether the specified key has been modified. $keyvalidation Identify whether a primary or candidate key requires validation. $totkeys Return the total number of keys for an entity.
